title: 30-Day Beginner Sadhana
summary: A minimal daily practice sustainable for a full month. Designed for a beginner building a habit — roughly 15 minutes a day.

steps.title: Morning (10 minutes)
steps.instruction: Rise before sunrise where possible. Wash. Light the diya. Sit before the altar. Do achamana. Chant one mala (108) of your chosen mantra.

steps.title: Before meals
steps.instruction: Silently offer the food to the deity with the 'brahmārpaṇaṁ' verse or simply 'oṃ namo nārāyaṇāya' three times before eating.

steps.title: Evening (5 minutes)
steps.instruction: Light the diya at dusk. Perform a brief aarti. If time permits, read a few verses from the Bhagavad Gita, the Vishnu Sahasranama, or a short stotra in translation.

steps.title: Weekly
steps.instruction: On the day of your ishta-devata (Monday — Shiva, Tuesday — Hanuman / Devi, Wednesday — Ganesha, Thursday — Vishnu / Guru, Friday — Devi / Lakshmi, Saturday — Shani / Hanuman, Sunday — Surya), extend the morning japa to three malas and the evening reading to a longer passage.

steps.title: Monthly
steps.instruction: Observe one Ekadashi fast (see the ekadashi-fasting guide). Visit a temple on a new moon or full moon day if convenient.

steps.title: Notice and adjust, don't force
steps.instruction: After 30 days, assess: what is sustainable? Keep that. A small steady practice beats an ambitious one that is abandoned.

sources.quote: Yoga is not for one who eats too much or too little, nor for one who sleeps too much or too little...
